hero.title: 3 Days in Central Vietnam.
hero.titleAccent: Basket Boats, Coconut Forest & UNESCO Ancient Town.
hero.intro: Central Vietnam's essential pairing in three affordable days. Da Nang's beaches — one of Asia's most beautiful coastlines. Then a day in Hoi An: the basket boat (coracle) experience through the Bay Mau coconut palm wetland, and a guided walk through the UNESCO-listed Ancient Town where 500-year-old merchant houses line the lantern-lit river.
hero.heroImageAlt: Hoi An Ancient Town lanterns UNESCO Japanese Bridge Vietnam

includes: Basket boat ride at Bay Mau coconut forest
Hoi An Ancient Town guided walk with entry fee

overview.heading: 3 Days Central Vietnam: Danang & Hoi An
body: This three-day package pairs Da Nang — a coastal city with excellent flight connections, beautiful beaches, and a growing cultural scene — with a full day at Hoi An, one of Southeast Asia's most beautifully preserved historic towns. It is the most efficient way to experience Central Vietnam's two most visited destinations.

Day two is the heart of the package: the Bay Mau coconut palm wetland experience followed by a guided walk through Hoi An Ancient Town. Bay Mau is a tidal wetland 3 kilometres outside Hoi An where local fishermen still work in traditional coracles — round woven bamboo boats traditionally used on these waterways. Fishermen demonstrate their distinctive foot-rowing technique, perform a boat-spinning routine, and guide you through the narrow coconut-palm-shaded waterways. The Hoi An singing and dancing on the river is an optional performance that the boatmen often offer spontaneously.

Hoi An Ancient Town, 30 kilometres south of Da Nang, was the most important international trading port in Southeast Asia from the 15th to the 18th centuries. Japanese merchants, Chinese traders, Dutch and Portuguese seafarers all established quarters in the town. The result is an architectural layering that is unique in Vietnam — and the entire Old Town has been preserved as a UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1999.

highlights.heading: Why This 3-Day Central Vietnam Package Stands Apart

items.title: Basket Boat at Bay Mau Coconut Forest
items.body: The traditional coracle — a round woven bamboo boat sealed with resin — is one of the most distinctive craft of the Hoi An fishing community. The Bay Mau coconut palm wetland provides the perfect setting: narrow water channels through dense nipa palm forest, where local fishermen demonstrate their extraordinary foot-rowing technique and the spinning manoeuvre that requires years of practice. The boatmen often break into karaoke singing mid-channel, spontaneously, which is both absurd and wonderful. Fishermen also demonstrate net casting and crab trapping in the wetland channels.
items.imageAlt: Bay Mau coconut palm basket boat coracle Cam Thanh Hoi An Vietnam

items.title: Hoi An Ancient Town — UNESCO Walking Tour
items.body: Hoi An's Old Town preserves the architecture of a 15th-century Asian trading port almost completely intact. The Japanese Covered Bridge (Lai Vien Kieu), built in 1593 by the Japanese merchant community to connect to the Chinese quarter across a small canal, is the most-photographed sight in Hoi An — a wooden bridge with a small pagoda above it, curved at both ends in the Japanese style. The guided walk also covers a traditional Fujian Chinese Assembly Hall (1757), the Tan Ky Old House (200-year-old Chinese-Japanese hybrid merchant house), and the Museum of Trade Ceramics.
items.imageAlt: Hoi An Ancient Town Japanese Covered Bridge UNESCO Vietnam lanterns

items.title: Da Nang Beach — One of Asia's Most Beautiful Coastlines
items.body: My Khe Beach, stretching 30 kilometres along Da Nang's coast, was named one of the most attractive beaches in the world by Forbes magazine. The beach begins within walking distance of Da Nang city centre, with gentle waves, clear water, and a long flat shoreline that was used by the US military as a rest and recreation area during the Vietnam War — for reasons that are immediately obvious. The water is warm (27–29°C year-round), the beach is wide, and it is practical in a way that tourist-focused resort beaches often are not.
items.imageAlt: My Khe Beach Da Nang coastline white sand Vietnam

days.title: Arrive Danang — Check In & Beach
days.description: Arrive at Da Nang International Airport, met by your Dhesu representative and transferred to your hotel. Free afternoon to check in and explore at your own pace. My Khe Beach is within walking distance or a short taxi ride from most central hotels. The beach is safe for swimming and uncrowded on weekday afternoons. Evening at leisure for self-arranged dinner — the area around Bach Dang Street along the Han River has excellent seafood restaurants.
suggestedActivities: Da Nang Airport arrival and hotel transfer

days.title: Hoi An — Basket Boat & UNESCO Ancient Town
days.description: 09:00–10:30: Car and guide collect guests from hotel for the Bay Mau Coconut Forest basket boat experience — traditional coracle (bamboo boat) ride through the nipa palm waterways of the Cam Thanh wetland, with local fishermen demonstrating net casting, crab fishing, boat spinning, and karaoke singing on the water. 11:30–12:00: Transfer to Hoi An (30 km, 45 minutes). Lunch at local restaurant. 13:30: Guided walking tour of Hoi An Ancient Town with entry fees included: Japanese Covered Bridge (1593), Tan Ky Old House (200-year-old merchant house), and the Old Town's lantern-lit streets and market. Free time for shopping — Hoi An's tailors, lantern makers, and silk traders are the best in Vietnam. 16:00–17:30: Return to Da Nang.
suggestedActivities: Bay Mau basket boat ride (coracle experience)
Fishermen net casting and crab fishing demo
Boat spinning and karaoke on the water
Hoi An Ancient Town free time (tailors, lanterns, market)

days.title: Danang Free Morning — Depart
days.description: Free morning in Da Nang for self-arranged activities. Options: Marble Mountains (five marble and limestone hills with Buddhist shrines and hilltop views, 20 minutes south of the city), Han Market (Da Nang's traditional daily market with local produce, textiles, and street food), or a final beach walk. Transfer to Da Nang International Airport per your agreed schedule.
suggestedActivities: Optional: Marble Mountains (Buddhist shrines and hill views)

whyBook.heading: Central Vietnam Made Easy — By Malaysia's Trusted Agency Since 1988
whyBook.body: Dhesu has been arranging Central Vietnam journeys for Malaysian travellers since 1988. The Danang–Hoi An circuit is one of our most popular short-break itineraries. Our guides know the Bay Mau boatmen who give the most authentic coracle experience, the Hoi An tailor shops that deliver on time, and the Da Nang restaurant streets that give you the real local food rather than the tourist menu versions.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. Central Vietnam is one of our most booked circuits — we understand every detail from basket boats to Hoi An entry tickets.

points.title: 35+ Years of Vietnam Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been sending Malaysian travellers to Vietnam. Our Da Nang and Hoi An guides are trusted long-term partners.

points.title: Basket Boat & Hoi An Included
points.body: The Bay Mau basket boat ride and Hoi An Ancient Town guided walk (with entry fees) are included in the package. No separate ticketing or transport arrangements needed.

points.title: Best Value in Central Vietnam
points.body: RM688 per person for a 4-star superior hotel, Hoi An day trip, basket boat, and all transfers — the most affordable way to experience Central Vietnam from Malaysia.

sections.heading: 3 Day Danang Hoi An Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
A 3-day Da Nang and Hoi An tour from Malaysia is the most accessible way to experience Central Vietnam on a short break. Da Nang is directly connected to Kuala Lumpur by multiple flights daily (approximately 2.5 hours). Hoi An, 30 kilometres south, is one of Southeast Asia's most photographed and most intact historic towns — a UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1999.

The basket boat experience at Bay Mau Coconut Forest (also called Cam Thanh) is a distinctively Hoi An experience that most Central Vietnam tours include — the traditional coracle fishing culture of the Thu Bon River wetlands is not replicated anywhere else in Vietnam. The combination of the coracle ride and the Hoi An walking tour makes day two one of the most varied and visually rich single days available in a Southeast Asia package tour.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Vietnam?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days. Arrive at Da Nang International Airport with your Malaysian passport — no advance application required.

items.question: What is the basket boat experience?
items.answer: The basket boat (coracle) is a traditional circular woven bamboo boat sealed with resin that has been used by the fishing communities of the Hoi An river delta for centuries. The Bay Mau Coconut Forest experience involves riding through the narrow nipa palm waterways with a local fisherman who demonstrates the foot-rowing technique, net casting, crab trapping, and boat-spinning. It is suitable for all ages and fitness levels — the boats are stable, the water is calm, and no physical exertion is required from guests.

items.question: How far is Hoi An from Da Nang?
items.answer: Approximately 30 kilometres south of Da Nang city centre, taking about 45 minutes by private vehicle. The drive passes My Khe Beach and the Marble Mountains. Your guide and vehicle are included for the Hoi An day.

items.question: Can I get clothes tailor-made in Hoi An in one day?
items.answer: Yes, for simple garments. Hoi An's tailor shops can produce simple items (shirts, trousers, dresses) in 4–8 hours with a fitting. For more complex items (suits, ao dai), same-day delivery is possible if you place the order in the morning and collect in the afternoon. For the best results, bring photographs or a garment you want copied. Your guide can recommend tailors with a reliable same-day delivery record.
